era_true.statement: Aperture Science was founded by Cave Johnson.
Cave Johnson originally sold shower curtains before founding Aperture Science.
GLaDOS stands for Genetic Lifeform and Disk Operating System.
Chell is the protagonist of both Portal and Portal 2.
The Aperture Science Handheld Portal Device creates two linked portals on flat surfaces.
One portal is blue and the other is orange.
GLaDOS flooded the Enrichment Center with a deadly neurotoxin.
The cake promised to test subjects by GLaDOS was a lie.
Companion Cubes are weighted storage cubes with hearts on each face.
Chell was forced to incinerate her Companion Cube in Portal.
GLaDOS was activated for the first time during Aperture Science's bring-your-daughter-to-work day.
Wheatley is a personality core designed to generate bad ideas.
Wheatley was attached to GLaDOS to make her less intelligent.
Cave Johnson died from moon rock poisoning.
Caroline was Cave Johnson's assistant.
GLaDOS was built using Caroline's consciousness.
Cave Johnson ordered his engineers to put Caroline into a computer.
Aperture Science is located in Upper Michigan.
The facility extends deep underground into old salt mines.
Turrets in Aperture Science speak in polite, childlike voices.
Turrets fire bullets at test subjects who enter their line of sight.
The Material Emancipation Grill vaporizes unauthorized equipment carried through it.
Ratman, also known as Doug Rattmann, was an Aperture Science employee who survived GLaDOS's neurotoxin attack.
Rattmann left cryptic drawings and messages on hidden walls throughout the facility.
Rattmann placed Chell's name at the top of the test subject list.
Rattmann was schizophrenic and relied on medication to manage his condition.
Portals cannot be placed on moving surfaces in standard test conditions.
Momentum is conserved when traveling through portals.
GLaDOS refers to this momentum conservation as "speedy thing goes in, speedy thing comes out."
Repulsion gel is blue and causes objects to bounce off surfaces.
Propulsion gel is orange and causes objects to slide faster across surfaces.
Conversion gel is white and allows portals to be placed on any surface it coats.
All three gels were originally created by Aperture Science.
Repulsion gel was originally marketed as a dietary aid meant to be ingested.
Cave Johnson recorded messages that play throughout the old Aperture testing spheres.
The old Aperture facility contains test chambers from the 1950s, 1970s, and 1980s.
Wheatley takes over the facility by replacing GLaDOS in her chassis.
GLaDOS is compressed into a potato battery after Wheatley takes control.
Potato GLaDOS is carried on the portal gun by Chell through old Aperture.
Wheatley becomes corrupted and power-mad after being placed in GLaDOS's body.
Chell defeats Wheatley by placing a portal on the surface of the moon.
The vacuum of space pulls Wheatley through the portal and into lunar orbit.
GLaDOS deletes Caroline from her system after regaining control of the facility.
GLaDOS releases Chell to the surface at the end of Portal 2.
A turret opera plays as Chell rides the elevator to the surface.
Chell emerges into a wheat field at the end of Portal 2.
The Companion Cube is ejected to the surface alongside Chell.
GLaDOS was originally designed to be a fuel system de-icing protocol.
Black Mesa is a rival company to Aperture Science.
Cave Johnson expressed deep resentment toward Black Mesa in his recordings.
Aperture Science competed with Black Mesa for government contracts.
The Borealis is an Aperture Science research vessel that disappeared with part of its dry dock.
The Borealis contained experimental Aperture Science technology.
Atlas and P-body are cooperative testing robots built by GLaDOS.
Atlas is the short, round blue-eyed robot.
P-body is the tall, orange-eyed robot.
GLaDOS built Atlas and P-body to replace human test subjects.
Atlas and P-body can be destroyed and reassembled indefinitely.
GLaDOS discovered a vault of human test subjects in cryogenic storage through Atlas and P-body's testing.
The Aperture Science Enrichment Center contains numerous test chambers.
Test chambers are designed to evaluate the use of the portal device.
Thermal Discouragement Beams are lasers used in test chambers.
Discouragement Redirection Cubes can redirect Thermal Discouragement Beams.
Aerial Faith Plates launch objects and people through the air.
Hard Light Bridges are solid walkways made of light.
Excursion Funnels are tractor beams that move objects and people.
Pneumatic Diversity Vents transport objects through tubes in the facility.
GLaDOS monitors all test chambers through cameras mounted on walls.
The cameras in the facility resemble small red-eyed devices on wall fixtures.
GLaDOS frequently insults Chell's weight and intelligence.
GLaDOS calls Chell an orphan and mocks her lack of family.
The Party Escort Bot was mentioned at the end of Portal.
Chell was dragged back into the facility by the Party Escort Bot after escaping.
GLaDOS sings "Still Alive" during the credits of Portal.
"Still Alive" confirms GLaDOS survived her destruction at the end of Portal.
GLaDOS and Wheatley sing "Want You Gone" during the credits of Portal 2.
The announcer voice in the facility is distinct from GLaDOS.
Aperture Science created the portal technology using moon rocks.
Moon rocks are an excellent portal-conducting surface.
Cave Johnson purchased seventy million dollars worth of moon rocks.
The moon rock purchase contributed to Aperture Science's financial decline.
Aperture Science tested on homeless people when they could no longer attract astronauts and war heroes.
Early Aperture test subjects included astronauts, war heroes, and Olympians.
Cave Johnson promised test subjects sixty dollars for their participation.
The Enrichment Center has relaxation vaults where test subjects are kept in stasis.
Chell was kept in a relaxation vault for an extremely long period between Portal and Portal 2.
The facility is heavily overgrown with vegetation at the start of Portal 2.
Wheatley accidentally wakes Chell at the beginning of Portal 2.
Wheatley tries to help Chell escape the facility before encountering GLaDOS.
GLaDOS is reactivated when Chell and Wheatley accidentally enter her chamber.
GLaDOS immediately recognizes Chell upon reactivation.
GLaDOS forces Chell to resume testing after being reactivated.
The corrupted cores attached to Wheatley include the Space Core, Adventure Core, and Fact Core.
The Space Core is obsessed with going to space.
The Adventure Core attempts to flirt with Chell.
The Fact Core recites mostly incorrect facts.
Attaching corrupted cores to Wheatley initiates a core transfer procedure.
A stalemate associate is needed to press a button to complete the core transfer.
Wheatley booby-traps the stalemate button with bombs.
Wheatley's test chambers are poorly designed and dangerous.
Wheatley attempts to kill Chell using crushing plates and spike traps.
GLaDOS admits that Caroline was the core of who she was.
Frankenturrets are turrets fused with cubes created by Wheatley.
The defective turrets in the facility do not fire properly.
GLaDOS replaces the working turrets with defective ones to sabotage Wheatley.
GLaDOS also sabotages Wheatley's neurotoxin supply using laser redirection.
The facility's reactor begins a meltdown under Wheatley's incompetent management.
Aperture Science's motto references the phrase "We do what we must because we can."
The Aperture Science logo resembles a stylized aperture or camera shutter.
Cave Johnson's portrait hangs in the old Aperture offices.
Caroline appears alongside Cave Johnson in a portrait in old Aperture.
GLaDOS reacts emotionally when hearing Cave Johnson's recordings about Caroline.
Potato GLaDOS generates approximately 1.1 volts of electricity.
A bird pecks at Potato GLaDOS and carries her away at one point.
GLaDOS later uses that same bird to threaten Atlas and P-body.
The long fall boots protect Chell from damage when falling from great heights.
Chell wears an orange Aperture Science jumpsuit.
The portal gun is officially called the Aperture Science Handheld Portal Device.
Only surfaces coated with portal-conductive material can sustain portals.
Metal and dark surfaces generally cannot hold portals.

era_false.statement: GLaDOS was built by Black Mesa as a competitor to their own AI systems.
Chell is the biological daughter of Cave Johnson and Caroline.
The portal gun can create up to three linked portals simultaneously.
Wheatley was designed as Aperture's most intelligent core to oversee all testing.
Atlas and P-body are human test subjects placed inside robotic shells.
The Companion Cube contains a living human being trapped inside it.
Cave Johnson survived his illness and continued to run Aperture Science personally.
GLaDOS was activated for the first time during the events of Portal 2.
Rattmann successfully escaped the Aperture Science facility before the neurotoxin event.
The orange portal and blue portal have different physical properties from each other.
Chell speaks frequently throughout both Portal games during gameplay and cutscenes.
Aperture Science was originally founded as a shower curtain manufacturer in the 1940s.
The moon surface cannot sustain a functioning portal in the Portal universe.
GLaDOS voluntarily released Chell at the end of the first Portal game.
Wheatley was created by Cave Johnson before GLaDOS was ever designed.
The turrets in Aperture Science are manufactured by Black Mesa under contract.
Chell defeats GLaDOS in Portal 2 by incinerating her in a furnace.
The Borealis is an Aperture Science ship that appears physically in the Portal games.
Cave Johnson died from exposure to repulsion gel rather than moon rock poisoning.
GLaDOS floods the facility with water instead of neurotoxin to kill test subjects.
The portal gun was invented by a Black Mesa scientist who defected to Aperture.
Wheatley becomes remorseful immediately after taking control of the facility from GLaDOS.
The cooperative testing initiative features three robots working together in teams.
Aperture Science is located beneath the streets of New York City.
GLaDOS's personality was based on Cave Johnson's mind uploaded into the system.
Chell wears the long fall boots to increase her running speed.
The cake promised by GLaDOS is eventually given to Chell as a reward.
Portal 2 takes place approximately five years after the events of Portal.
The Space Core was specifically designed to navigate Aperture's underground facilities.
Doug Rattmann is a former Aperture security guard who went into hiding.
GLaDOS was designed with the ability to feel physical pain from the beginning.
Chell uses a gravity gun alongside the portal device in Portal 2.
The Enrichment Center is located on the surface level of the Aperture facility.
Wheatley successfully manages the facility without any problems after taking over.
Caroline volunteered enthusiastically to have her mind uploaded into GLaDOS.
The turrets fire rockets instead of bullets at test subjects.
Aperture Science won every science fair competition against Black Mesa.
Portal takes place before the events of Half-Life in the shared timeline.
The Fact Core provides exclusively accurate and verified information throughout Portal 2.
GLaDOS deletes Caroline's personality from her system at the start of Portal 2.
Chell was an Aperture Science employee before becoming a test subject.
The repulsion gel was originally developed as a dietary pudding substitute.
Conversion gel is blue and allows portals to be placed on any surface.
Atlas is the taller orange-eyed robot in the cooperative testing mode.
GLaDOS maintains a friendly and supportive tone with Chell throughout Portal 1.
The Aperture Science Handheld Portal Device was the company's first successful product.
Wheatley speaks with an American accent throughout Portal 2.
Cave Johnson recorded his messages during the 2000s shortly before the Portal events.
The defective turrets are more dangerous than the standard production turrets.
Chell destroys Wheatley at the end of Portal 2 to save the facility.
GLaDOS was the only artificial intelligence ever created by Aperture Science.
The pneumatic diversity vents transport test subjects between different test chambers.
Aperture Science and Black Mesa maintained a close collaborative partnership.
The portal gun requires ammunition that must be collected throughout the facility.
P-body is the shorter blue-eyed robot in the cooperative testing mode.
GLaDOS rewards successful test subjects with freedom from the Enrichment Center.
The excursion funnel is a solid bridge made of hard light panels.
Chell has been confirmed to be in her fifties during Portal 2.
Wheatley was attached to GLaDOS's body as an intelligence enhancement core.
The thermal discouragement beam is a stream of freezing cold energy.
Cave Johnson founded Aperture Science in the 1980s during the tech boom.
GLaDOS controls the facility using voice commands given to human workers.
The aerial faith plates launch test subjects downward through the floor.
Rattmann's murals depict cheerful scenes of life outside the Aperture facility.
Chell defeats GLaDOS in Portal by solving a final extremely difficult test chamber.
The propulsion gel makes surfaces bouncy so test subjects can reach high areas.
Wheatley is eventually recovered from space and returned to the facility.
GLaDOS was shut down multiple times before the events of the first Portal game.
The cooperative testing robots can use a single shared portal gun between them.
Aperture Science is a subsidiary of Black Mesa and operates under their authority.
Cave Johnson's portrait shows him as a young man with glasses and a beard.
The neurotoxin is described as having a pleasant floral scent in the games.
Chell carries a backpack containing supplies throughout her journey in the facility.
GLaDOS promises to reunite Chell with her family if she completes all tests.
The hard light bridges can be passed through portals without losing their structure.
Wheatley was one of dozens of identical personality cores mass-produced by Aperture.
The announcer voice heard in old Aperture belongs to a core named Craig.
Turrets self-destruct after failing to hit their target within ten seconds.
The Curiosity Core asks questions about the outside world and current events.
GLaDOS runs on a nuclear reactor located deep beneath the main facility.
Chell obtains the portal gun by stealing it from a defeated turret.
The elevator rides between test chambers are optional in both Portal games.
Cave Johnson's recordings reveal he was a humble and cautious businessman.
Repulsion gel is orange and increases the speed of anyone who walks on it.
GLaDOS was originally programmed to be a military defense system.
The personality cores were created to make GLaDOS more aggressive and efficient.
Wheatley helps Chell escape the facility at the end of Portal 2.
The Aperture Science logo features a flame inside a hexagonal shape.
Chell wears an orange jumpsuit with Aperture branding throughout both games.
GLaDOS sings a duet with Wheatley during the Portal 2 credits sequence.
The test chambers in Portal are located on the surface in outdoor environments.
Cave Johnson explicitly states that Caroline is his wife in his recordings.
The portal gun can create portals on any surface regardless of material.
Rattmann dies on screen during the events of the first Portal game.
The cooperative testing campaign takes place before the single-player story of Portal 2.
GLaDOS transforms into a human body during the climax of Portal 2.
Chell is mute because GLaDOS surgically removed her ability to speak.
The Enrichment Center contains a fully functioning city for Aperture employees.
Wheatley designed the test chambers that Chell navigates in the early parts of Portal 2.
The Adventure Core is obsessed with cooking and shares recipes during Portal 2.
GLaDOS was built in a single day according to Aperture Science records.
Portals remain open permanently once placed and cannot be closed or moved.
Chell finds other living human survivors inside the facility during Portal 2.
The bird that appears in Portal 2 is revealed to be a robotic spy.
Cave Johnson's final recording instructs scientists to shut down GLaDOS immediately.
Aperture Science is located in Michigan beneath a salt mine in the Upper Peninsula.
GLaDOS expresses genuine happiness when Wheatley takes control of the facility.
The Companion Cube can be carried between test chambers throughout the entire game.
Wheatley was programmed by Black Mesa as a sabotage agent inside Aperture.
The old Aperture testing spheres are located above the modern facility.
Chell uses the portal gun to defeat both bosses by shooting portals directly at them.
GLaDOS's chassis allows her to move freely throughout the entire facility.
The turret opera at the end of Portal 2 is performed by human singers.
Cave Johnson became president of the United States before founding Aperture Science.
The gel substances were invented by GLaDOS after she took control of the facility.
Rattmann's companion cube actually speaks to him with an audible voice in the games.
Portal 2 ends with Chell choosing to remain inside the Aperture facility voluntarily.
The cores attached to GLaDOS were designed to enhance her testing capabilities.
Wheatley apologizes sincerely to Chell before being pulled into space.
GLaDOS reveals that the entire facility is located on the moon's surface.

modern_true.statement: The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye.
Honey does not spoil when stored in sealed containers.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
Water expands when it freezes into ice.
Australia is both a country and a continent.
The human body contains more bacterial cells than human cells.
Light from the Sun takes about eight minutes to reach Earth.
The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's surface.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramids.
Bananas are naturally radioactive due to their potassium content.
The Amazon River is the largest river by volume of water discharge.
Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in the Solar System.
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440.
DNA has a double helix structure first described by Watson and Crick in 1953.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain above sea level on Earth.
Sharks existed before trees evolved on Earth.
The speed of light in a vacuum is 299,792,458 meters per second.
Russia spans eleven time zones.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The Mariana Trench is the deepest known point in Earth's oceans.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
The human genome contains roughly 20,000 protein-coding genes.
Iceland runs almost entirely on geothermal and hydroelectric energy.
The Roman Empire fell in 476 AD in the West.
Tardigrades can survive the vacuum of outer space.
Gold is chemically inert and does not rust or corrode under normal conditions.
The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as the United States.
Cows have four stomach compartments.
The Rosetta Stone was discovered in 1799 in Egypt.
Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.
Human trafficking remains a global crisis affecting millions of people.
The Pacific Ocean is larger than all landmasses on Earth combined.
Galileo Galilei was placed under house arrest for supporting heliocentrism.
Elephants are the largest living land animals.
The French Revolution began in 1789.
Lightning strikes the Earth about 100 times every second.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft has left the Solar System's heliosphere.
Greenland is the world's largest island that is not a continent.
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred on April 26, 1986.
Diamonds are made of carbon atoms arranged in a crystal lattice.
The Nile River flows northward through northeastern Africa.
Nepal is home to eight of the world's fourteen peaks above 8,000 meters.
The transistor was invented at Bell Labs in 1947.
Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins can.
The Ottoman Empire lasted from 1299 to 1922.
Antarctica has no permanent human residents.
The human brain consumes about 20 percent of the body's total energy.
India has the second largest population of any country on Earth.
The speed of sound in air at sea level is about 343 meters per second.
Neutron stars are so dense that a teaspoon of their material would weigh billions of tons.
The Magna Carta was signed in 1215 in England.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean.
The first successful powered airplane flight occurred at Kitty Hawk in 1903.
Mars has the tallest known volcano in the Solar System, Olympus Mons.
The human eye can distinguish roughly ten million different colors.
South Africa has three capital cities.
The Black Death killed roughly one-third of Europe's population in the 14th century.
Helium is the second most abundant element in the observable universe.
Lake Baikal in Russia is the deepest freshwater lake on Earth.
The first photograph was taken by Joseph Nicéphore Niépce in 1826.
Coral reefs support roughly 25 percent of all marine species.
The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure visible from orbit.
The Apollo 11 mission landed humans on the Moon on July 20, 1969.
Platypuses are venomous mammals that lay eggs.
The Internet's foundational protocol TCP/IP was standardized in 1983.
Sanskrit is one of the oldest known languages with a written record.
The Earth's inner core is primarily composed of iron and nickel.
Greenland's ice sheet contains enough water to raise global sea levels by about seven meters.
The first vaccine was developed by Edward Jenner in 1796 against smallpox.
There are more possible chess games than atoms in the observable universe.
The Suez Canal opened in 1869 connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea.
Mantis shrimp can perceive ultraviolet and polarized light.
The human skeleton has 206 bones in adulthood.
The Atacama Desert in Chile is the driest non-polar desert on Earth.
The first electronic general-purpose computer, ENIAC, was completed in 1945.
Trees communicate through underground fungal networks called mycorrhizae.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into orbit in 1990.
Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa.
Glass is an amorphous solid, not a liquid.
The Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after hitting an iceberg.
Mercury is the smallest planet in the Solar System.
The human body is about 60 percent water by mass.
The Treaty of Westphalia in 1648 established the modern concept of state sovereignty.
Crows can recognize individual human faces and remember them for years.
The Large Hadron Collider is the world's largest and most powerful particle accelerator.
Finland has more saunas than cars.
The Gutenberg Bible was the first major book printed with movable type in Europe.
Earthquakes on the Moon are called moonquakes.
The Amazon Rainforest produces about six percent of the world's oxygen.
The first email was sent by Ray Tomlinson in 1971.
Blue whales are the largest animals known to have ever existed on Earth.
The Himalayas are still rising in height due to tectonic plate collision.
Vatican City is the smallest internationally recognized independent state by area.
The Periodic Table has 118 confirmed elements.
The Rwandan genocide occurred in 1994 and killed roughly 800,000 people in 100 days.
Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen using sunlight.
The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway line in the world.
The Wright brothers' first flight lasted twelve seconds.
Seahorses are the only animals in which the male becomes pregnant and gives birth.
The Voyager Golden Record contains sounds and images representing life on Earth.
The Andes is the longest continental mountain range on Earth.
The first human heart transplant was performed by Christiaan Barnard in 1967.
Earth's magnetic poles have reversed many times throughout geological history.
The Galápagos Islands inspired Charles Darwin's theory of natural selection.
Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago nation with over 17,000 islands.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast about 100,000 slices of bread.
The Hagia Sophia in Istanbul has served as a cathedral, mosque, museum, and mosque again.
Neutrons and protons are made of smaller particles called quarks.
The Braille writing system was invented by Louis Braille in 1824.
The Moon is slowly moving away from Earth at about 3.8 centimeters per year.
The Rosetta spacecraft's Philae lander touched down on a comet in 2014.
Ethiopia follows a calendar that is seven to eight years behind the Gregorian calendar.
Mitochondria have their own DNA separate from the cell's nuclear DNA.
The Strait of Malacca is one of the most important shipping lanes in the world.
The first programmable computer was the Z3, built by Konrad Zuse in 1941.
Flamingos are born white and turn pink from carotenoid pigments in their diet.
The deepest point ever reached by humans in the ocean is Challenger Deep.
